channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pakistan says a new round of peace talks with Afghanistan is underway in China
item.description: Pakistan confirmed it was holding peace talks with Afghanistan's Taliban government in China, where Beijing is trying to broker a lasting ceasefire after weeks of fighting.
item.content:encoded:
Pakistan confirmed it was holding peace talks with Afghanistan's Taliban government in China, where Beijing is trying to broker a lasting ceasefire after weeks of fighting.

(Image credit: Siddiqullah Alizai)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuba releasing 2,010 prisoners as the US pressures the island's government
item.description: The Cuban government said the pardons were a "humanitarian gesture" in connection with Holy Week and didn't mention mounting pressures with the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The Cuban government said the pardons were a "humanitarian gesture" in connection with Holy Week and didn't mention mounting pressures with the U.S.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.K. convenes 40 nations to discuss Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
item.description: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff as the Iran war rages through Week 5
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras/AFP)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
item.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
item.description: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.
item.content:encoded:
The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

(Image credit: Ariana Cubillos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
item.description: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.
item.content:encoded: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.
item.dc:creator: Scott Tong

item.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: A Turkish border town known for its cats - in times of peace
item.description: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers - and its cats.
item.content:encoded: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers - and its cats.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng
